← All worlds/World 12
World 12 · 1 state

Emotional Growth & Maturity

Films for the slow accumulation of becoming — the small wisdoms that don’t announce themselves, the quiet evidence that you’ve grown.

Choose an emotion
Becoming
On Becoming

Growth does not announce itself. It shows up in retrospect — a conversation you handled better than you would have two years ago, a reaction you no longer have, a version of yourself you remember with something approaching compassion. You became this person slowly, without noticing, through a thousand ordinary days. Cinema is one of the few art forms that can render that slowness faithfully — the accumulation of small changes that adds up to someone different. These films are about the long arc. The patience. The quiet arrival of a self that is less afraid and more honest than the one you started with.

Becoming

Films for the long arc you didn’t notice you were on.

CS8.4
2013

About Time

Richard Curtis

View Film →

A young man learns the men in his family can travel in time — and slowly discovers the gift isn’t about changing the past but paying attention to the present.

CS9.2
2013

Before Midnight

Richard Linklater

View Film →

Eighteen years after a night in Vienna, Jesse and Celine are together, parents, on holiday in Greece — and a long evening starts to test what they’ve built.

CS9.0
2019

Marriage Story

Noah Baumbach

View Film →

A theatre director and an actress navigate a coast-to-coast divorce, trying to remain themselves through a system designed to flatten them.

CS8.8
2014

Boyhood

Richard Linklater

View Film →

A boy’s life from age six to age eighteen, filmed across twelve real years with the same cast — a fiction that aged in real time.

CS9.0
2019

Little Women

Greta Gerwig

View Film →

The four March sisters become themselves alongside each other in nineteenth-century New England, in a re-imagining that intercuts childhood and adulthood.

CS8.8
2020

Minari

Lee Isaac Chung

View Film →

A Korean-American family moves to rural Arkansas in the 1980s to start a farm. The film is a slow, attentive portrait of building a life in unfamiliar soil.

CS8.7
2016

Paterson

Jim Jarmusch

View Film →

A bus driver in Paterson, New Jersey, named Paterson, writes poems between routes. The film follows seven days of his quiet, devoted life.

CS9.3
2023

Perfect Days

Wim Wenders

View Film →

A Tokyo public-toilet cleaner lives a routine of cassette tapes, paperbacks, and small kindnesses. The film follows him quietly through several days.

CS8.7
2023

The Holdovers

Alexander Payne

View Film →

A grumpy classics teacher, a troubled student, and a grieving school cook are stranded together at a New England boarding school over Christmas 1970.

CS9.1
1997

Good Will Hunting

Gus Van Sant

View Film →

A self-taught janitor at MIT, secretly a mathematical prodigy, is taken on by a math professor and a therapist who refuse to let him sabotage his future.

CS9.2
1959

The 400 Blows

François Truffaut

View Film →

A twelve-year-old Parisian boy, misunderstood at home and at school, drifts toward delinquency. Truffaut’s autobiographical debut.

CS9.3
2001

Spirited Away

Hayao Miyazaki

View Film →

A ten-year-old girl becomes trapped in a spirit world and must work in a bathhouse to free herself and her parents.

CS8.8
1986

Stand By Me

Rob Reiner

View Film →

Four twelve-year-olds walk along railway tracks to find a dead body. Told by one of them as an adult.

CS9.0
2000

Almost Famous

Cameron Crowe

View Film →

A fifteen-year-old secretly writes for Rolling Stone in 1973 and goes on tour with a fictional rock band.

CS9.0
2017

The Florida Project

Sean Baker

View Film →

A six-year-old outside Disney World spends a summer in chaos while her mother struggles to survive.

CS8.5
2000

High Fidelity

Stephen Frears

View Film →

A thirty-something record store owner in Chicago reviews his romantic failures through the framework of his top-five lists.

CS8.4
2007

Juno

Jason Reitman

View Film →

A sixteen-year-old navigates an unplanned pregnancy with wit and deeply examined emotional intelligence.

CS8.4
2013

The Spectacular Now

James Ponsoldt

View Film →

A popular but directionless high school senior falls for a quiet girl. Both are changed.

CS9.0
2013

Short Term 12

Destin Daniel Cretton

View Film →

A twenty-something supervisor at a foster care facility manages her own unprocessed trauma alongside her wards’.

CS8.8
2019

Rocks

Sarah Gavron

View Film →

A fifteen-year-old in East London becomes suddenly responsible for her younger brother when their mother disappears.

CS8.9
1993

Dazed and Confused

Richard Linklater

View Film →

The last day of high school in 1976 Austin. Fifteen characters, one long afternoon.

CS8.9
2018

Eighth Grade

Bo Burnham

View Film →

A thirteen-year-old YouTuber tries to survive the last week of middle school.

CS9.0
2017

Lady Bird

Greta Gerwig

View Film →

A senior in a Sacramento Catholic high school fights with her mother, dreams of New York, and tries to figure out which version of herself is real.

CS8.8
1985

The Breakfast Club

John Hughes

View Film →

Five high school students serve Saturday detention and reveal who they actually are.

Obsession →

For the thing that took over and wouldn’t let go.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.

18 emotional states·277 curated films·Growing weekly
